title: Self-evaluation
statusLabel: Live — evaluation workspace and management tools shipped
journey: School self-evaluation and inspection readiness usually lives across shared documents, spreadsheets, evidence folders, and ad-hoc follow-up meetings — hard to keep current and harder to hand over. This feature gives a school one structured workspace to record judgements against a real inspection framework (starting with the UAE framework, with others planned), assign sections of the review to the right staff member, and turn findings directly into tracked follow-up work. The plan for this one went through five rounds of independent review before being accepted, and the build is now live: recording judgements, delegating sections, setting deadlines, and approving and locking finished sections all work end-to-end, with a few extras — like a second framework and a fuller evidence library — still to come.

subfeatures.name: Framework & standards model
subfeatures.note: The underlying data model for inspection frameworks, domains, and rating scales — live and holding real judgements.

subfeatures.name: Judgement recording
subfeatures.note: Capturing a rating and rationale for each criterion, at the right level of detail for each standard. A section owner's entries are saved as drafts until a review lead approves them.

subfeatures.name: Section assignment
subfeatures.note: Delegating whole standards — or narrower slices of them — to the right staff member, with a coverage view showing what's unowned or overdue.

subfeatures.name: Permissions & access
subfeatures.note: Who can record, approve, or manage which parts of the self-evaluation is now enforced by the database itself. Finer control over who can view the review is still to come.

subfeatures.name: UAE framework content
subfeatures.note: The official UAE School Inspection Framework, pre-loaded and ready to use.

subfeatures.name: IB PSP framework content
subfeatures.note: The IB Primary Years self-study framework, as a second supported standard — deferred until after the current release.

subfeatures.name: Review workspace
subfeatures.note: The main screen: work through each standard, see where the review stands at a glance, and switch into a dedicated management view.

subfeatures.name: Section sign-off & locking
subfeatures.note: A review lead can approve a section's work and lock it read-only; when every section is locked, the whole review counts as complete, and a locked section can only be reopened deliberately.

subfeatures.name: Deadlines
subfeatures.note: Set a termly deadline for each section of the review, with overdue sections flagged in the coverage view.

subfeatures.name: Follow-up tasks
subfeatures.note: Turn a finding into a tracked task that links straight back to the relevant part of the review.

subfeatures.name: Report export
subfeatures.note: Export the self-evaluation as a Word or PDF document.

subfeatures.name: Evidence attachments
subfeatures.note: Links and file uploads can be attached to a criterion today; a fuller evidence library is planned for a later release.

testing.fixtureNote: The shared fixture now seeds a realistic working review: an active cycle plus an archived one from a prior year, owners assigned across all six sections (including one narrower subject-and-phase delegation), a draft judgement awaiting approval, one section already approved and locked, an open follow-up task, an attached piece of evidence, a termly deadline, and the school's year-group-to-phase mapping — covering the main shapes this feature needs to handle.

expectedOutcomes.expected: The framework title, progress at a glance, and the tabs for evaluating, reviewing the overview, and managing the review all appear — in both the quick-preview mode and the real signed-in mode.

expectedOutcomes.criterion: A judgement lands in the right place
expectedOutcomes.expected: Saving a rating and rationale records it for exactly that subject and phase, and the progress display updates — no ratings are ever invented for scopes nobody judged.

expectedOutcomes.criterion: Only the right people can record judgements
expectedOutcomes.expected: A review lead records judgements directly; a section owner's entry is saved as a draft awaiting approval; anyone else is refused with a clear on-screen message rather than a silent failure.
Once a section is approved and locked, nobody — including the review lead — can change it until it is deliberately unlocked.

expectedOutcomes.criterion: One school can never see another's review
expectedOutcomes.expected: Signed in at one school, reading or changing another school's review data returns nothing and is refused — including indirect routes that point at another school's records.
